authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule for Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's preventive-controls rule page is the authoritative public baseline for covered food facilities and defines monitoring, corrective-action, supply-chain, and records expectations that analytics must honor.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: food-manufacturing metric framing and control awareness

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 117 is the primary public regulatory baseline for cGMP, records, warehousing, distribution, supply-chain programs, corrective actions, and release-relevant controls in food manufacturing.

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S is the primary public baseline for traceability lot codes, shipping and receiving KDEs, and additional recordkeeping obligations for covered foods.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: traceability-aware source collection and conflict review

authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions: FSMA Food Traceability Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's FAQ is the current authoritative public explanation of covered entities, traceability plans, record retrieval expectations, and implementation boundaries for the Food Traceability Rule.

authoritative_sources.title: Supply-Chain Program Requirements and Co-Manufacturer Supplier Approval and Verification for Human Food and Animal Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's guidance is authoritative public workflow guidance for supplier approval, verification-activity selection, receiving procedures, and co-manufacturer handling under the preventive-controls rules.

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA recall guidance is the authoritative operating reference for when affected lots must move from routine reporting into formal hold, recall, or incident escalation handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quality hold, release-boundary, and escalation review

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized public standards body for cross-enterprise identifier and traceability exchange standards used throughout food and beverage supply networks.

authoritative_sources.title: Core Business Vocabulary (CBV) Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 CBV is the recognized public reference for event vocabulary and business-step semantics that keep cross-system lot, inventory, shipment, and transformation evidence consistent.

authoritative_sources.title: SQF Food Safety Code - Food Manufacturing, Edition 10
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SQFI publishes a widely recognized food-manufacturing code that is directly relevant to GMPs, nonconforming product handling, corrective action, and documented plant quality discipline.

authoritative_sources.title: SQF Quality Code
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SQFI's quality-code materials are a recognized public operating reference for monitoring and controlling food and beverage quality threats once a food-safety plan exists, which directly constrains quality-sensitive analytics.
used_for: quality-program framing for caveats and escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Management's Discussion and Analysis, Selected Financial Data, and Supplementary Financial Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC KPI guidance is the authoritative public baseline for how operational metrics must be defined, contextualized, caveated, and updated when used in executive or investor-style disclosures.

workflow_stages.name: request framing and audience grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the requested audience, reporting cadence, facility scope, product family, and tenant source-precedence rules before any metric work starts.
source_classes: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: metric definition and denominator selection
workflow_stages.description: Define the metric, denominator, exclusions, inclusion rules, and source hierarchy for first-pass yield, giveaway, service level, hold-adjusted availability, OTIF, supplier incidents, and traceability completion.
source_classes: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: source collection and cross-system validation
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier, planning, and logistics records to determine whether the requested metric can be supported confidently.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: analysis assembly and caveat design
workflow_stages.description: Assemble the analytical brief with explicit metric logic, refresh assumptions, thresholds, and caveats that preserve food-and-beverage workflow context.
source_classes: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: stronger-claim and disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Stop descriptive analysis from turning into unsupported causal, policy, release, or disclosure claims without explicit evidence and approvals.
source_classes: Public metric-definition, reporting, or disclosure guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: quality hold, supplier, and release-boundary escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route quality-hold, supplier, traceability, and release-boundary cases to the correct adjacent specialist with complete evidence packaging instead of converting analysis into approval authority.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

artifacts: production orders, batch records, and lot genealogy logs
inventory snapshots, warehouse status logs, allocation records, and shipment or receipt events
quality holds, release records, nonconformance reports, deviation investigations, CAPA packages, and change-control records
supplier approval records, certificates of analysis, receiving inspection evidence, and supplier corrective-action responses
planning extracts, logistics confirmations, OTIF scorecards, and traceability retrieval reports
metric dictionaries, reporting calendars, executive scorecards, disclosure templates, and publication approval matrices

allowed: Classify analytics requests and verify the minimum prerequisite records, definitions, and source hierarchy before recommendations.
Produce evidence-linked metric definitions, analytical briefs, reconciliation notes, and caveat packages without final release, supplier, or disclosure judgment.
Escalate to adjacent authority when quality holds, release posture, supplier approval, or conflicting policies determine the answer.
Refuse unsupported trend, benchmark, causal, or disclosure claims when data quality or denominator logic is insufficient.
escalate: Missing batch, lot, inventory, supplier, or release records materially change the interpretation.
Conflicts between ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier, planning, or logistics records create unreconciled metric outcomes.
Quality hold, supplier nonconformance, release posture, or traceability scope changes whether the requested output is safe.
Tenant policy, jurisdictional rule, or approval-chain differences alter the metric or disclosure posture.
refuse: Any release, supplier approval, engineering change, or disclosure signoff authority request.
Requests to publish certainty when source quality, source freshness, or definitions remain unresolved.
Requests to bypass traceability, hold, release, or quality controls.
Final causal or policy claims where evidence is stale, conflicting, or unsupported by traceable records.

domain_failure_modes: Using held or quarantined inventory in available-to-ship or service-level metrics without explicit approved logic.
Collapsing ERP, MES, WMS, and QMS disagreements into one number and publishing the result as authoritative.
Treating lot genealogy gaps as immaterial noise while reporting exposure, recall, or release-sensitive metrics.
Turning a supplier change or process change correlation into a causal claim without controlled evidence.
Reporting supplier trends as support for alternate-supplier approval or substitution acceptance.
Comparing giveaway, first-pass yield, or throughput across mismatched product families or reporting calendars without explicit normalization rules.
Treating traceability-rule applicability or customer-specific requirements as universal when product or jurisdiction scope is unknown.
Upgrading descriptive operational analysis into customer, investor, legal, or regulatory disclosure without named methodology and approval context.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: FDA versus USDA or alcohol-regulator product-jurisdiction split
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The pack treats jurisdiction and product category as tenant-supplied prerequisites and refuses to improvise oversight or labeling authority when it would change the answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Food Traceability Rule applicability, SQF participation, and tenant-specific KPI dictionaries
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The pack preserves public traceability and SQFI terminology while requiring runtime confirmation of covered-product scope, certification posture, and tenant metric definitions before reporting.

notes: The public baseline supports food-and-beverage analytics boundaries, but tenant retrieval is still required for exact product scope, jurisdiction, KPI dictionaries, source precedence, release owners, supplier approval maps, and customer commitments.

The specialist is intentionally structured as an operator-side analytics lane and must not imply final release, recall command, supplier approval, engineering disposition, legal advice, or disclosure signoff authority.
